Lot was good, but there was no one else like him in the city. All the people of Sodom were very wicked and vile. Care was therefore taken for saving Lot and his family. Lot lingered; he trifled. Thus many who are under convictions about their spiritual state and the necessity of a change delay that needful work. The salvation of the most righteous person is due to God's mercy, not by their own merit. We are saved by grace. God’s power also must be acknowledged in bringing souls out of a sinful state. If God had not been merciful to us, our lingering would have been our ruin. Lot must flee for his life. He must not long for Sodom. Such commands as these are given to those who, through grace, are delivered out of a sinful state and condition. Do not return to sin and Satan. Rest not in self and the world. Reach toward Christ and heaven, for that is escaping to the mountain, short of which we must not stop. Concerning this destruction, observe that it is a revelation of God’s wrath against sin and sinners of all ages. Let us learn from here the evil of sin and its hurtful nature; it leads to ruin.

See the peril of security. Lot, who remained chaste in Sodom, and was a mourner for the wickedness of the place, and a witness against it, when on the mountain, alone, and as he thought out of the way of temptation, was shamefully overtaken. Let him that thinks he stands high and firm take heed lest he fall. See the peril of drunkenness; it is not only a great sin itself, but lets in many sins which bring a lasting wound and dishonor. Many a man does things when he is drunk that he could not think of without horror when he is sober. See also the peril of temptation, even from relations and friends whom we love and esteem and expect kindness from. We must dread a snare wherever we are and be always upon our guard. No excuse can be made for the daughters nor for Lot. Scarcely any account can be given of the affair but this: The heart is deceitful above all things, and desperately wicked; who can know it? From the silence of Scripture concerning Lot afterward, learn that drunkenness, as it makes men forgetful, so it makes them to be forgotten.
